observations.topic: Recall gap: '/'-qualification requirement misses genuine bare broken references
observations.detail: Independently confirmed methodology/06_release_train.md:72 contains a bare (non-'/'-qualified) reference `05_release_train.md` (inside the phrase '`00_planning.md` through `05_release_train.md` were, at initial framework genesis') naming a file that no longer exists post the methodology renumbering -- genuine rename drift, structurally the same class of narrative-truth bug C9/F-026 exists to catch. Because check_c9_path_resolution's extraction regex requires a '/' before the extension-bearing segment, this token is never even extracted as a candidate and C9 will silently pass over it forever. Independently re-derived the contract's own extraction regex and dry-ran it against the full shipped-doc set + docs/guide/index.html: confirmed this bare token yields zero regex matches, while every genuine directory-qualified reference in the corpus (docs/architecture/ADR-*.md, methodology/NN_*.md, schema/*.schema.json, tools/*.md, .agent/contracts/NNN.json, etc.) is correctly extracted. Also independently confirmed the contract's false-positive rationale for requiring '/': a from-scratch bare-extension-word scan of the same corpus turned up genuine class-(a) cross-repo terms (SCOPE.md, ECOSYSTEM.json, DEPENDENCY_MAP.md in standard/cross_repo_governance.md and tools/SKILL.md -- none exist in this repo), class-(b) same-directory relative markdown-link targets (`[skill.json](skill.json)`, `[interface.md](interface.md)` in tools/README.md; `[NDS.md](NDS.md)`, `[GIP.md](GIP.md)` etc. in standard/README.md -- none resolve against repo-root CWD), and class-(c) ASCII-tree illustrative leaf labels in docs/guide/index.html's <pre> blocks (bare `NNN.json`, `provenance.json`, `run_state.json`, `feature_list.json` -- none exist at repo root) -- all of which a bare-word sweep would false-FAIL on. Verdict: the '/' requirement is a defensible, evidence-backed tradeoff (it does not gut C9's mission -- every phase-003 H1/H4/H5-class finding the spec cites as C9's motivation was directory-qualified, and the false-positive corpus is large and real) but it IS a real, currently-demonstrable recall gap, not merely a theoretical one, and the contract's own text ('confirmed zero coverage loss against the corpus's own genuine directory-qualified cross-references') is true only for the '/'-qualified subset and could be misread as a broader claim. Recommend the contract (or a follow-on DEBT.md entry, logged by the orchestrator, outside this contract's scope) explicitly name methodology/06_release_train.md:72 as a known, accepted C9 blind spot rather than leaving the tradeoff implicit. Separately, CHANGELOG.md carries directory-qualified stale `methodology/05_release_train.md` references (lines 103, 177) that C9 will also never catch, but for an unrelated reason: CHANGELOG.md is not a member of the shipped-doc set or the guide and is never swept by C9 in any mode -- a scope gap, not a '/'-qualification gap. Non-blocking: does not affect the soundness of AT2's non-breaking claim (independently reproduced 'SUMMARY: 9 passed, 0 failed' / 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 8 passed, 0 failed' with the two contracted fixes applied, and exactly the two claimed unresolved tokens without them).

observations.topic: AT4's `vlib_path_resolves` composition check is a bare substring grep in isolation
observations.detail: Verification entry 4 asserts `grep -q 'vlib_path_resolves' tools/validate.sh`, which in isolation could be satisfied by a dead comment mentioning the function name without ever invoking it (a literal-substring anti-pattern per methodology/02 Sec 10(d)). Not flagged as blocking because entries 5, 6, 10, and 11 independently force genuine content-driven, function-anchored behavior: entry 5 requires the FAIL fixture to produce a non-zero exit AND name the exact offending token; entry 6 requires the PASS fixture to report [C9] PASS; entries 10/11 re-target neutral mktemp-renamed copies of both fixtures, which would defeat any filename-keyed stub and require the underlying resolution logic to actually run per-file. A content-blind stub that merely mentioned vlib_path_resolves in a comment would fail entries 5/6/10/11. Recommend entry 4 be hardened in a future revision to assert the token appears inside the check_c9_path_resolution function body specifically (e.g. via vlib_section_grep once the function exists), but this is not blocking given the functional coverage elsewhere.

observations.topic: Attribution of the methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md guide-enumeration DEBT note
observations.detail: design_notes.pre_existing_finding and non_goals both state the guide's omission of methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md from its 'methodology/' key-documents list is 'logged as a DEBT observation' but the contract's scope.files_modify does NOT include docs/planning/DEBT.md, and verification entry 8's vlib_scope_guard allow-list (tools/validate.sh, tools/fixtures/, tools/interface.md, docs/guide/index.html) would REJECT a Generator edit to docs/planning/DEBT.md as out-of-scope (DEBT.md is not under .agent/ and matches none of the four allowed prefixes). Read literally as an instruction to the Generator, this is self-contradictory. Resolved this as a non-blocking observation on the reading that 'logged as a DEBT observation for the orchestrator' means the orchestrator/evaluator layer records this awareness item (as this review now does), not that feature 026's Generator scope includes editing DEBT.md. Recommend the orchestrator log this gap to docs/planning/DEBT.md itself, outside contract 026's scope, once this contract is implemented.

observations.topic: Independent confirmation of the two C9-specific additional exemptions
observations.detail: Confirmed `.nizam/` genuinely does not exist anywhere in the framework's own working tree (test -d .nizam -> absent), directly parallel to the already-adopted tools/.claude//tools/.codex/ precedent (confirmed present in tools/README.md line 34). Confirmed `.agent/capability_profile.json` and `.agent/debt.json` do not exist in this repo's .agent/ directory (ls .agent/ shows no such files) and are present in schema/README.md's 'Validates' column (lines 43-44) as artifact-shape descriptions, not as claims this repo's own .agent/ contains them; confirmed this repo's own debt register is docs/planning/DEBT.md (exists, frontmatter-valid) rather than .agent/debt.json. Neither exemption masks a real, fixable broken reference; both are legitimate structural-absence exemptions.

final_verdict.summary: Contract 026 (C9 repo-wide path-resolution check) survives adversarial testability review. All seven adversarial targets were independently re-derived and dry-run rather than taken on the contract's word: (1) the exact extraction+exemption regex was rebuilt from the design_notes prose and run over the real default shipped-doc-set-union-guide corpus and the real payload-doc-set corpus; WITHOUT the two contracted fixes it reproduces exactly the two claimed unresolved tokens (tools/interface.md:46, docs/guide/index.html:535, both 'methodology/05_release_train.md'), and WITH the fixes applied (in-memory only, no real file touched) it yields ZERO unresolved tokens in both modes, independently reproducing the current tree's baseline (8 passed / 7 passed payload via `bash tools/validate.sh` / `--payload`) plus exactly one new passing check in each mode -- substantiating the claimed 'SUMMARY: 9 passed, 0 failed' / 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 8 passed, 0 failed' non-breaking outcome. (2) The '/'-qualification recall tradeoff is real (confirmed methodology/06_release_train.md:72's bare, genuinely-broken '05_release_train.md' reference will never be extracted) but is an evidence-backed, non-mission-gutting design choice -- independently confirmed a bare-word sweep would false-fail on real cross-repo terms, same-directory relative-link targets, and ASCII-tree illustrative labels present in this corpus; logged as an observation, not a blocking defect. (3) Both C9-specific additional exemptions ('.nizam/' prefix; the two named schema-README singleton tokens) were independently confirmed structurally-absent-by-design, not masking a fixable bug. (4) The anti-cheat fixture design (entries 10/11 re-targeting neutral mktemp-renamed copies of both fixtures) genuinely defeats a filename-keyed stub, and entry 5 forces the FAIL output to name the real offending token. (5) All 15 verification commands were executed against the current, unimplemented tree: the 9 that test C9-specific behavior (1,2,4,5,6,7,12,13,14) all correctly FAIL now (proving they are not vacuously pre-satisfied), while the 6 that assert invariants expected to hold both before and after implementation (3, 8, 9, 15, plus the two components of 13/14 confirming no premature edits exist) correctly PASS now for the right reasons -- no whole-file/vacuous grep was found to false-pass on the unedited tree. (6) vlib_scope_guard is confirmed untracked-aware (git status --untracked-files=all) and will see the new fixtures and reject anything outside the four allow-listed paths. (7) The scope expansion to tools/interface.md and docs/guide/index.html is independently confirmed NECESSARY (not merely convenient) for AT2's non-breaking gate to hold, and fixing the content rather than adding a masking exemption is the correct call -- exempting the literal stale token would hide exactly the class of drift bug C9 exists to catch. Three non-blocking observations are logged (the recall-gap disclosure, AT4's standalone substring weakness, and the DEBT-logging attribution ambiguity for the guide's methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md enumeration gap) for the orchestrator's awareness; none gate approval.
